1.LLM Internals :
LLM Internals = How a Large Language Model works on the inside.
This chapter teaches the main building blocks of modern AI models like ChatGPT.
It covers:
🔹Transformers → The basic architecture that powers LLMs.
🔹Attention → How the model decides which words are important.
🔹MoE (Mixture of Experts) → Uses different specialized parts of the model for different tasks, making it faster and more efficient.
🔹RoPE → Helps the model understand the order and position of words.
🔹GQA → An improved attention method that reduces memory usage while keeping performance high.
🔹KV Cache → Stores previous calculations so text generation becomes much faster.
🔹Inference Scaling → Techniques used to make models respond faster and more efficiently in real-world applications.
2. The Transformer Revolution:
Before 2017, AI models like RNNs and LSTMs read text one word at a time.
This caused two problems:
🐢 Slow training → They had to wait for one word before reading the next.
🧠 Poor memory → They struggled to remember words from far earlier in a sentence.
Then, in 2017, the Transformer architecture was introduced in the paper "Attention Is All You Need."
Instead of reading words one by one, a Transformer looks at all the words in a sentence at the same time using self-attention.
Transformers made AI much faster and much better at understanding long sentences by processing all words in parallel instead of one by one.
3.Architecture Variants :
There are 3 types of Transformer architectures, each designed for a different purpose.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Transformer Architectures1. Encoder-only (Understanding)
Looks at the entire sentence at once.
Great for understanding meaning.
Used for:
Text classification
Sentiment analysis
Named Entity Recognition (NER)
Creating embeddings
Example: "Is this review positive or negative?"
2. Decoder-only (Most modern LLMs)
Reads text from left to right.
Predicts the next word one by one.
Perfect for:
Chatbots
Code generation
Story writing
Text completion
Example: ChatGPT keeps predicting the next word until the answer is complete.
Why it's popular:
✅ Simple architecture
✅ Easy to train
✅ Scales well
✅ Best for text generation
3. Encoder-Decoder (Read → Write)
Encoder first understands the input.
Decoder then generates the output.
Best for:
Translation
Summarization
Question answering
Example:
Input: "Translate 'Hello' to French."
Output: "Bonjour"
4.Mixture of Experts (MoE):
MoEThink of a hospital:
👨‍⚕️ Instead of one doctor treating every patient, there are many specialists (heart doctor, skin doctor, eye doctor, etc.).
🧑‍💼 A receptionist decides which specialists a patient should see.
That's exactly how MoE works.
In an LLM:
Experts = Many small specialized neural networks.
Router = Decides which expert(s) should process each token (word).
Instead of every expert working on every word, only the best 1 or 2 experts are chosen.
Why is this useful?
⚡ Faster → Only a few experts do the work.
💰 Less computation → Saves processing power.
🧠 Larger models → You can have many experts without using all of them every time.
Example
Sentence:
"Write Python code to sort a list."
The router might choose:
✅ Coding Expert
✅ Python Expert
It doesn't activate:
❌ Translation Expert
❌ Math Expert
Total vs Active Parameters
Imagine a company with 1,000 employees.
Total parameters = All 1,000 employees exist.
Active parameters = Only 20 employees work on your task.
So:
The model is very knowledgeable (many experts exist).
But it's fast, because only a few experts work for each token.
One-line summary
Mixture of Experts (MoE) makes an LLM smarter and faster by using a router to activate only the most relevant experts for each word, instead of using the entire model every time.
